Title: Cellulose synthase-like protein OsCSLD4 plays an important role in the response of rice to salt stress by mediating abscisic acid biosynthesis to regulate osmotic stress tolerance

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: expression is reduced in oscsld4 mutant and increased in OsCSLD4 overexpression lines, consistent with contribution to ABA accumulation
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: repressed in nd1 under normal and salt stress
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: repressed in nd1; induced in OsCSLD4 overexpression plants
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: ABA promotes osmotic stress tolerance under salinity
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsNCED4 is a key ABA biosynthesis gene whose transcript level is strongly decreased in nd1. 2. Its expression is enhanced in OsCSLD4 overexpression plants. 3. It serves as major evidence that OsCSLD4 promotes ABA biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: described by authors as a key ABA synthesis gene

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: expression is decreased in nd1, supporting reduced ABA biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: ABA promotes osmotic stress tolerance under salinity
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsNCED1 transcript is approximately half of WT in nd1. 2. This supports impaired ABA biosynthesis downstream of OsCSLD4.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: background/supporting gene rather than primary experimental focus

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: expression is decreased in nd1, supporting reduced ABA biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: ABA promotes osmotic stress tolerance under salinity
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsAAO1 transcript is reduced in nd1. 2. This supports the conclusion that OsCSLD4 positively affects ABA biosynthetic capacity.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: supporting gene rather than core subject

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: expression is decreased in nd1, supporting reduced ABA biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: ABA promotes osmotic stress tolerance under salinity
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsAAO2 transcript is reduced in nd1. 2. It supports reduced ABA biosynthesis in the OsCSLD4 mutant.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: supporting gene rather than core subject

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: expression is decreased in nd1, supporting reduced ABA biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: ABA promotes osmotic stress tolerance under salinity
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsSDR2 transcript is reduced in nd1. 2. It provides additional support that ABA synthesis is impaired when OsCSLD4 function is disrupted.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: supporting gene rather than core subject

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ABA-responsive regulatory gene expression is impaired in nd1
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsbZIP23 expression is markedly impaired in nd1. 2. This indicates weakened ABA-responsive transcription downstream of reduced ABA levels.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: not a core gene of this paper; included as supporting ABA-response marker

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ABA/stress-responsive regulatory gene expression is impaired in nd1
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: salt stress and normal condition analyses
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: stress adaptation downstream of ABA-related signaling
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsDREB1C expression is impaired in nd1. 2. This supports the conclusion that OsCSLD4 disruption weakens ABA/stress-responsive gene expression.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: supporting downstream regulatory marker, not primary focus

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ABA-responsive gene expression is reduced in nd1 and elevated in overexpression lines
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: lower in nd1, higher in OsCSLD4 OE
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: lower in nd1, higher in OsCSLD4 OE
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsRAB16C is downregulated in nd1 and upregulated in OsCSLD4 OE. 2. It is used as a representative ABA-responsive output gene supporting OsCSLD4-mediated ABA action.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ABA-responsive gene expression is markedly impaired in nd1
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsRAB17 expression in nd1 is about 10% of WT. 2. It supports compromised ABA-responsive output in the mutant.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ABA-responsive function gene expression is impaired in nd1
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsLIP9 is downregulated in nd1. 2. It supports impaired ABA-responsive stress gene activation in the mutant.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ABA-responsive function gene expression is markedly impaired in nd1
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsRD22 expression in nd1 is about 19% of WT. 2. It supports severely impaired ABA-responsive transcription in the OsCSLD4 mutant.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: ABA biosynthesis-mediated osmotic stress tolerance pathway; cell wall polysaccharide biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: cell wall polysaccharide synthesis linked to ABA-mediated salt response
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: catalyzes synthesis of cell wall polysaccharides and thereby positively mediates expression of ABA biosynthetic genes and ABA accumulation under salt stress
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: indirectly promotes target product accumulation by supporting ABA biosynthetic gene expression
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: upstream indirect controller of ABA accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes ABA accumulation, osmotic stress tolerance, salt tolerance, and increases grain width and grain weight
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: loss-of-function mutant analysis (nd1, nrl1); RNAi knockdown; overexpression; ABA quantification; qRT-PCR of ABA biosynthesis/response genes; salt, PEG, LiCl and ABA treatments; transcriptome analysis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: expressed in roots, stems and leaves; rapidly induced by 150 mM NaCl within 1 h and remains high until 8 h; slightly induced by ABA
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: expressed in roots, stems and leaves; salt inducible
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 150 mM NaCl salt stress; 10% PEG osmotic stress; 18 mM LiCl; exogenous ABA
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: promising target for engineering rice with improved salt tolerance and higher grain weight/yield without evident penalty reported; overexpression is beneficial
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: loss of function causes narrow and rolled leaves, dwarfism, reduced grain width and reduced 1000-grain weight; no overexpression penalty reported
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: maize Ubiquitin promoter (constitutive, for RNAi); pCAMBIA1307 overexpression construct promoter not explicitly stated
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: ABA promotes osmotic stress adaptation and salt stress tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (Illumina HiSeq 2500; 12 libraries); no accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsCSLD4 is specifically induced by salt stress in rice. 2. Disruption of OsCSLD4 lowers ABA content, represses ABA biosynthesis and ABA-responsive genes, and reduces salt/osmotic stress tolerance. 3. Exogenous ABA rescues the salt-sensitive phenotype of nd1. 4. Overexpression of OsCSLD4 increases ABA content, enhances salt tolerance, and improves grain width and 1000-grain weight.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Salt-sensitive phenotype is attributed to impaired osmotic stress tolerance rather than altered ion transport or ROS scavenging. Salt-induced OsCSLD4 expression is largely independent of ABA-induced expression.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: cellulose synthase-like D family glycosyltransferase; CSLD family membrane polysaccharide synthase
